title: Chemistry Test 2 #CT002 (Atomic Structure, Transformation and Inorganic)

questions.text: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists. List-I (Characteristic): (a) Zero mass (b) Fractional charge (c) Fractional spin (d) Integral spin. List-II (Particle): 1. Positron 2. Neutrino 3. Quark 4. Photon
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: (A) 2 3 1 4**

**Correct Matching:**
(a) Zero mass → Neutrino (2)
(b) Fractional charge → Quark (3)
(c) Fractional spin → Positron (1)
(d) Integral spin → Photon (4)

**Concept Explanation:**
Modern particle physics classifies particles based on properties like mass, charge and spin.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
(A) Correct mapping according to particle physics.
(B) Incorrect because neutrino has near-zero mass.
(C) Incorrect because photon corresponds to integral spin.
(D) Incorrect matching of quark and neutrino.

**Key DRDO Exam Insight:**
• Quarks have fractional charges (+2/3, −1/3).
• Photon has spin = 1.
• Neutrinos have extremely small mass and no electric charge.

questions.text: Which of the following particles has zero mass?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

**Explanation:**
Photons are particles of electromagnetic radiation. According to modern physics they have **zero rest mass** but possess energy and momentum.

**Option Analysis:**
Electron – Has measurable mass (9.11 × 10⁻³¹ kg).
Neutrino – Very small but non-zero mass.
Photon – Massless particle.
Proton – Heavy particle in nucleus.

**Exam Tip:**
Massless particles in physics include **photons and gluons**.

questions.text: Which particle possesses fractional electric charge?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Quark**

**Explanation:**
Quarks are fundamental particles forming protons and neutrons. They possess **fractional charges such as +2/3 or −1/3**.

**Option Analysis:**
Electron – Charge −1.
Proton – Charge +1.
Quark – Fractional charge.
Neutron – Net charge zero.

**Exam Insight:**
Six types of quarks exist: up, down, charm, strange, top, bottom.

questions.text: Which particle has integral spin?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

**Explanation:**
Particles are classified into:
• **Fermions** (half-integer spin)
• **Bosons** (integer spin)

Photon is a **boson with spin = 1**.

**Option Analysis:**
Electron – Spin 1/2
Quark – Spin 1/2
Neutrino – Spin 1/2
Photon – Spin 1

questions.text: Which particle is nearly massless and electrically neutral?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Neutrino**

**Explanation:**
Neutrinos are extremely light neutral particles produced during nuclear reactions such as beta decay.

**Exam Insight:**
Neutrinos rarely interact with matter and can pass through Earth without collision.

questions.text: Which fundamental particle is responsible for electromagnetic radiation?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

**Explanation:**
Photon is the quantum particle of electromagnetic radiation including visible light, radio waves and X-rays.

questions.text: Which particle is the antiparticle of electron?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Positron**

**Explanation:**
Positron has same mass as electron but opposite charge.

**Exam Insight:**
Electron + Positron annihilation produces gamma rays.

questions.text: Which particle is a constituent of proton and neutron?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Quark**

Protons and neutrons are composed of **three quarks each**.

questions.text: Which particle mediates electromagnetic force?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

Photons act as **force carriers of electromagnetic interaction**.

questions.text: Which of the following is a boson?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

Bosons have integer spin. Photon has spin = 1.

questions.text: Which of the following is a fermion?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Electron**

Fermions have half-integer spin and obey **Pauli Exclusion Principle**.

questions.text: Which particle carries no electric charge?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Neutron**

Neutron is electrically neutral.

questions.text: Which particle determines chemical properties of an atom?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Electron**

Electrons determine **chemical bonding and reactivity**.

questions.text: Which particle determines atomic number?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Proton**

Atomic number = number of protons.

questions.text: Which particle contributes most to atomic mass?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Proton and neutron**

Electrons have negligible mass.

questions.text: Which particle exists outside the nucleus?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Electron**

questions.text: Which particle was discovered by J.J. Thomson?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Electron**

questions.text: Which particle was discovered by Rutherford?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Proton**

questions.text: Which particle was discovered by James Chadwick?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Neutron**

questions.text: Which particle is responsible for nuclear stability?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Neutron**

Neutrons reduce repulsion between protons and stabilize the nucleus.
